compiled_state.title: As a sprint orchestrator, implementation-plan references the shared testing standard instead of maintaining a parallel rule set so testing requirements are consistent everywhere
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Replace implementation-plan's Behavioral Test Requirement section with a reference to the shared behavioral testing standard. Move the acceptable structural test categories that currently live in implementation-plan into the shared standard (done in the sibling story). Retain planning-specific items (unit exemption criteria, integration test rule, filename conventions) in implementation-plan as workflow orchestration rules. Update the test approach requirement to use Given/When/Then framing per the shared standard's Rule 2.
**Why**: Two parallel rule sets for behavioral testing create drift and inconsistency. The shared standard is the single source of truth; implementation-plan should reference it, not duplicate it.
**Scope**:
- IN: Replace Behavioral Test Requirement section in implementation-plan SKILL.md with a reference to the shared standard; reframe test approach requirement to Given/When/Then; keep unit exemption criteria, integration test rule, and filename conventions in implementation-plan
- OUT: Changes to behavioral-testing-standard.md (sibling story); changes to TDD plan reviewer (sibling story); changes to agent definition files

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, implementation-plan's Behavioral Test Requirement section is replaced with a reference to the shared standard and the parallel rule set is eliminated
  ← Satisfies: "Implementation-plan's Behavioral Test Requirement section is replaced with a reference to the shared standard; the parallel rule set is eliminated"
- When this story is complete, implementation-plan's test approach requirement uses Given/When/Then framing per the shared standard's Rule 2
  ← Satisfies: "Implementation-plan's test approach requirement uses Given/When/Then framing per the shared standard's Rule 2"
- When this story is complete, planning-specific items (unit exemption criteria, integration test rule, filename conventions) remain in implementation-plan as workflow orchestration rules
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Replacing the section heading could break plan parsers expecting that heading -- verify no downstream tooling references the heading name
- [Maintainability] Must be updated atomically with the shared standard to avoid inconsistent rule sets during the transition
